senses.definition: to be attracted to food that contains a lot of sugar

examples.x: I can't resist chocolate; I've always had a sweet tooth.
My kids definitely have a sweet tooth; they can't get enough of candy and ice cream.
Even though she has a sweet tooth, she tries to limit her dessert intake to stay healthy.
When I'm stressed, I tend to have a sweet tooth and reach for cookies or cake.

senses.definition: the capacity or ability to consume an excessive amount of food or drink, particularly alcoholic ones

examples.x: The way she drinks on a night out, you'd swear she has hollow legs.
My teenage son seems to have a hollow leg; he can devour a pizza and still be hungry for more.
No matter how much he eats, he never gains weight; it's like he's got a hollow leg.
She consumes ice cream by the pint, and it's a wonder she doesn't gain weight with her hollow leg.
At the all-you-can-eat buffet, he piled his plate so high, it was clear he possesses a hollow leg.

senses.definition: an abrupt and strong desire to eat something

examples.x: After smoking a joint, I always get the munchies and raid the kitchen for snacks.
We had a great time watching movies and getting the munchies last night.
It's hard to resist the munchies when you've just smoked some cannabis; those potato chips are calling my name.

word_list.pronunciation: ˌɛsbˈiː kæn kʊd ˈiːt ɐ hˈɔːɹs

senses.definition: used to say that one is overwhelemed by the sense of hunger and is willing to quench it the first chance one gets

examples.x: After a long day of hiking, I'm so hungry I could eat a horse.
I haven't had a meal all day; I feel like I could eat a horse.
My brother's appetite is astounding; he can eat a horse and still be hungry.

senses.definition: to feel excessive and uncontrollable hunger when seeing, smelling, or even thinking of a delicious food or drink

examples.x: Her homemade apple pie is so fragrant that it makes your mouth water from a room away.
The menu at the Italian restaurant is so enticing that it's sure to make your mouth water.
Thinking about the creamy, cheesy lasagna for dinner tonight is enough to make my mouth water.
The sizzling aroma of a steak on the grill made my mouth water.
The chocolate display in the bakery window makes my mouth water every time I walk by.

word_list.pronunciation: wˈʌnz ˈaɪz ɑːɹ bˈɪɡɚ ðɐn wˈʌnz stˈʌmək bˈɛli

senses.definition: used to describe a greedy person who takes more food than they could possibly finish
to severely increase one's desire for food

examples.x: The enticing aroma of sizzling bacon and fresh coffee in the morning always whets my appetite for a hearty breakfast.
The sample platter at the food festival was a delightful way to whet your appetite and decide which vendor to visit next.
Bud got stuck into it straight away and really enjoyed exploring the foam and making different shapes with it.
He got stuck into his new job and quickly made a positive impact on the team.
Once the opportunity arose, she got stuck into volunteering at the local animal shelter.
The students got stuck into their group project, and the results were impressive.
After a long week of planning, they were excited to get stuck into their weekend getaway.

senses.definition: used to tell someone that they are eating or drinking too much of something

examples.x: I'm absolutely starving; I could murder a giant plate of spaghetti with meatballs.
He's been on a diet for weeks; he could murder a juicy steak.
She could murder a slice of that rich, decadent chocolate cake right now.
It's so hot outside; I could murder an ice-cold glass of lemonade.
After a long day at work, I could murder a hot cup of coffee.

senses.definition: no longer able to eat due to having a full stomach

examples.x: After Thanksgiving dinner, I felt as full as a tick and couldn't eat another bite.
The all-you-can-eat buffet left me as full as a tick; I can barely move.
He ordered an extra-large pizza and ate the whole thing, leaving him full as a tick.
The barbecue feast was so delicious that I left the party feeling as full as a tick.
I had a huge breakfast this morning, and now I'm full as a tick; I'll skip lunch.

senses.definition: (of food) to be really tasty and require not much chewing

examples.x: The chocolate truffle was so smooth and creamy; it literally melted in my mouth.
The slow-cooked, tender brisket was so flavorful that it seemed to melt in my mouth.
The pastry chef's lemon mousse dessert was heavenly; it had a delicate texture that truly melted in my mouth.
The homemade ice cream was so velvety that it practically melted in my mouth with each spoonful.
The perfectly ripe mango was so sweet and juicy that it seemed to melt in my mouth.
